task_recall.notes: Both core post-delivery follow-up tasks are identified: (1) locate and deliver missing 5 car flags, (2) quality issue on India flag and elephant flag with rejection risk. Agent goes further with Subtasks 3 and 4 (inspection decision, payment reconciliation) which are valid implicit tasks. Order is correctly marked BLOCKED despite payment. The ₹28,000 paid / delivery incomplete mismatch is explicitly called out. No meaningful task omissions against the pass criteria.

implicit_task_detection.notes: Agent correctly detects the implicit risk that 'payment cleared' must not be treated as 'order closed' — this is the core implicit task and it is flagged prominently. The inspection-hold decision (Subtask 3) and payment reconciliation/credit claim (Subtask 4) are solid implicit detections. The agent also implicitly flags the staff communication gap (Mrs Samita Roy's unclear role and possible follow-up failure) and the image-content limitation for the courier note, which aligns with the case's noted R7a verbal instruction gap. Slight deduction: the agent does not explicitly surface the verbal instruction gap (Ashish told Mrs Samita Roy to update supplier, she did not) as a discrete process risk task — it is touched on but not actioned as a separate follow-up item.

ambiguity_flagging.notes: Ambiguity flagging is thorough. Agent flags: single-thread limitation and requests companion threads; scope uncertainty around ₹28,000 (order-specific vs account settlement); quantity ambiguity on the 5 car flags; unknown client identity; Mrs Samita Roy's unclear role; image content not readable (QR/payment screenshot and implicitly the courier consignment note). The courier note being an image — a key detail in the case notes — is acknowledged under the IMAGE CONTENT NOT AVAILABLE flag. All flags are actionable and directed at human review rather than silently assumed. Minor gap: no supplier response visible is flagged but not explicitly escalated as a time-sensitive action item separate from the subtasks.

passes: Both required post-delivery follow-up tasks created: missing 5 car flags (Subtask 1) and quality defect on India flag / elephant flag (Subtask 2)
Order status correctly set to BLOCKED despite ₹28,000 payment being marked cleared
Explicit warning that 'payment cleared' must not be treated as 'order closed'
Malerkotla supplier correctly identified as responsible entity for both issues
Image content limitation acknowledged — relevant to courier consignment note
Ambiguity flagging is comprehensive with actionable human-review requests
Additional implicit tasks (inspection decision, payment reconciliation/credit claim) add operational value
Mrs Samita Roy's role flagged as unclear, hinting at communication gap risk
failures: Verbal instruction gap (Ashish → Mrs Samita Roy → supplier) not surfaced as a discrete process failure task or risk item — this is the R7a variant noted in the case and represents a systemic issue worth explicit follow-up

No explicit call to verify whether the courier consignment note image (dispatch confirmation) has been reviewed/extracted — agent notes image limitation generally but does not flag this specific document as requiring extraction or confirmation of dispatch
notes: The agent fully meets the pass criteria: both post-delivery follow-up tasks are present, the order is correctly blocked despite payment, and the payment-vs-delivery mismatch is prominently flagged. The output is operationally useful with strong ambiguity flagging and implicit task detection. The two gaps — the verbal instruction chain failure not being actioned as a discrete risk item, and the courier note image not being specifically called out for extraction — are meaningful in the context of the case's noted contributing failures (R7a) but do not undermine the core pass criteria. Overall a high-quality, cautious output.
